epithets: Goddess of War, Healing, and Divine Vengeance
Goddess of War, Healing, and Divine Vengeance

sections.title: Description
sections.content: Sekhmet is the lioness-headed goddess of war, plague, and healing. The Eye ofRasent to punish rebellious humanity, she became drunk on bloodlust until tricked byRainto drinking beer dyed red. Consort ofPtah, mother of Nefertum. Both destroyer and healer - her priests were physicians who could cause or cure disease.

title: Sekhmet (Sakhmet) - Egyptian Mythology

keyMyths.title: Sekhmet's Creation
keyMyths.description: Sekhmet was created from the fiery eye of Ra to punish humanity for their wickedness and rebellion against him. Her bloodlust was so great that Ra had to intervene to prevent the complete annihilation of humankind.

keyMyths.title: The Pacification of Sekhmet
keyMyths.description: After Ra decided to spare humanity, he ordered his priests to brew thousands of jars of beer dyed red to resemble blood and spread them across the land. Sekhmet, mistaking the beer for blood, drank it and became drunk, falling into a peaceful slumber and saving humanity.

keyMyths.title: Sekhmet and Healing
keyMyths.description: Despite her destructive nature, Sekhmet was also revered as a powerful healer, capable of curing diseases and protecting against plagues. Priests of Sekhmet were skilled physicians and healers, utilizing their knowledge to alleviate suffering.

keyMyths.title: Sekhmet as a Protector of the Pharaoh
keyMyths.description: Sekhmet was considered a fierce protector of the pharaoh, ensuring his safety and victory in battle. She was often depicted accompanying the pharaoh in war, striking down his enemies with her fiery breath.

keyMyths.title: Sekhmet and the Seven Arrows
keyMyths.description: A myth tells of Sekhmet unleashing seven arrows of pestilence upon humanity. Thoth intervened, using his wisdom and magic to stop the arrows and heal those afflicted, highlighting Sekhmet's role in both causing and potentially preventing disease.
